Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

Patch Set 3:

> (2 comments)

I have a patch for use a typedef so that the code can be forward looking just as you have suggested.

Patch Set 3:

Before you update this patch can you move the autoconf test to libcfs/autoconf. It belongs their not in the lustre one.

Patch Set 3:

Also this can be directly based on the top of master instead for the header cleanups. That will help to land this faster.

Patch Set 3:

> Before you update this patch can you move the autoconf test to
 > libcfs/autoconf. It belongs their not in the lustre one.

The move to wait_queue_entry_t spans multiple directories, not just libcfs.  Does that influence your recommendation on the positioning of the probe?

Patch Set 3:

The way lustre is layered is libcfs -> lnet -> lustre

That is why I asked for the move to libcfs autoconf.

Patch Set 3:

> The way lustre is layered is libcfs -> lnet -> lustre
 > 
 > That is why I asked for the move to libcfs autoconf.

There was an issue getting LC_HAVE_ADD_WAIT_QUEUE_EXCLUSIVE working since it depends on HAVE_WAIT_QUEUE_ENTRY.

I solved that as follows:

        # 4.13
	LC_WAIT_QUEUE_ENTRY <--- by having this earlier than

	# 2.6.34
	LC_HAVE_DQUOT_FS_DISK_QUOTA
	LC_HAVE_DQUOT_SUSPEND
	LC_HAVE_ADD_WAIT_QUEUE_EXCLUSIVE <-- this

That made the generated configure script probe in the proper order.

Should I move both probes to the libcfg autoconf since they are both wait APIs?
Uploaded patch set 4.

parents.subject: LU-10560 lustre_compat: Convert GFP_TEMPORARY to GFP_KERNEL

commit.subject: LU-10560 libcfs: handle rename to wait_queue_entry_t
commit.message:
LU-10560 libcfs: handle rename to wait_queue_entry_t

The 4.13 kernel renames wait_queue_t to wait_queue_entry_t.

Add a probe and handle rename across the code base and have
a define to translate to the new name when indicated.

Test-Parameters: trivial
